name: create-diolog-guides
description: Build a multi-page, print-ready A4 guide in Diolog's brand design system as one self-contained HTML file that scrolls as a document on screen, exports to a clean A4 PDF, and carries screen-only micro-interactions that never reach the print. Use whenever the deliverable runs longer than one page: a guide, briefing, playbook, handbook, whitepaper, explainer, report, or 'turn this markdown into something we can send investors'. Sibling of create-diolog-infographic, which lands one finding on one page; this one sustains an argument across twenty, which is a systems problem rather than a composition problem. Mandates a design layer before any page exists (a spacing scale, a column grid, one shared baseline, print-first type, motion tokens), shapes the document into three to five page archetypes rather than twenty one-offs, and gates each archetype through design-craft's per-unit critique before reproducing it. Renders approved company-voice copy verbatim and builds all imagery as live HTML and CSS rather than raster. Ships a zero-dependency Node CDP harness that measures ink rather than boxes: guide-qa (cap-height alignment via baseline probe and canvas TextMetrics, optical gutters, stretch voids, orphan wrap rows, spacing-scale adherence, cross-page baseline drift, clamped-box centre drift, Range-based widows, WCAG AA contrast with alpha blended up the ancestor chain, plus per-component crops at DPR 3), scope-lint (selectors that cross a component boundary, dead rules, colliding global classes), page-fit (A4 fit plus the collisions overflow:hidden conceals, footer overlap, computed font families), motion-check (print and reduced-motion resting-state safety, then mid-flight frame capture), and export-pdf (verifies the produced PDF's page count, sheet size, link annotations, and that no transient animation label leaked into the ink). Carries an explicit verification epistemics: a gate is downstream of the findings that motivated it, so a clean gate means no known defect is present and never means verified; rendering a screenshot is not looking at one; and motion bugs exist only in mid-flight frames. Leans on design-craft and ux-craft (both blocking) and on create-luke-content or create-amy-content when copy must be authored rather than rendered. Not for a single-page infographic or poster (create-diolog-infographic), multi-slide decks (customer-deck-builder), email mockups (email-mockups), or app UI (design-craft). v1.0.1 documents the mixed-size-initial optical-centring pattern (a larger serif initial beside smaller mono caps rides high when baseline-aligned; nudge it down by half its cap-height surplus via transform) and two DOM ink-measurement traps (text-transform:uppercase means the measured string is not the rendered one; getComputedStyle metrics differ per font family so crop and look when the numbers fight you) - noting that the cap-misalign gate does not cover decorative initials, a live instance of the gate-sees-only-what-it-was-configured-for thesis. v1.1.0 adds the evidence-based emphasis-and-legibility layer from the July 2026 typography deep research: a binding per-page emphasis budget (italics display-only - the heading accent plus one isolated pull quote; micro-labels mono roman at or above the 11px floor; uppercase only for labels of five words or fewer; bold 1-2 phrases per paragraph), hierarchy via size steps and white space rather than font styling, row-major ordering for grouped sequences, pull quotes isolated outside the body flow, and the rule that this evidence outranks a master file's ad-hoc design notes - motivated by the italics overload and hierarchy flattening Amy's review caught in the shipped 30-questions guide.
